summary: The Docker-based reproduction builds the survey-pdf library from the pinned vulnerable commit, starts a simulated internal HTTP metadata service on 127.0.0.1:9999, creates a SurveyPDF instance with an image question whose imageLink points to that internal service, and confirms the library makes an outbound HTTP GET request to the attacker-specified internal URL.
expected_security_impact: The library should make an HTTP request to http://127.0.0.1:9999/ssrf-proof-metadata without any URL validation, demonstrating that an attacker can force the server to fetch arbitrary internal or external URLs through the survey JSON imageLink field.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned the target repository, built the library, launched the simulated internal service, created a SurveyPDF instance with the malicious imageLink, and the library made an HTTP GET request to http://127.0.0.1:9999/ssrf-proof-metadata. The simulated service received and logged the request, confirming the SSRF.
verification_method: The exploit script starts a local HTTP server on 127.0.0.1:9999 before invoking SurveyPDF. It sets a boolean flag when any request arrives. After PDF generation completes (or fails due to image processing), the script checks the flag. A true value confirms the library made an outbound request to the attacker-specified URL. The script distinguishes SSRF success from unrelated failures by checking the flag, not by checking PDF output quality.
evidence_produced_by_run: The simulated internal HTTP service on 127.0.0.1:9999 received a GET request to /ssrf-proof-metadata.
The request was initiated by the survey-pdf library's ImageUtils._getImageInfo() method in node.ts.
The library fetched the full response body into memory via res.arrayBuffer(), confirming the attacker can read internal service responses.
The SSRF occurred during normal survey PDF generation with no special configuration beyond the imageLink URL in the survey JSON.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository from GitHub on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
